req.title: Delete patient (soft) (negative)
req.statement: A system-admin shall soft-delete a patient resource; deletion shall not remove history. Negative paths including permission denial, missing mandatory elements, and conflicting state transitions must be tested.

doc.reasoning: R-HLT-054 mandates soft-delete of a Patient resource by an admin, with history preservation. The negative path requires 403 for non-admin callers. Tests are organized as: (1) Positive/EP – admin successfully soft-deletes an active patient and history is retained; (2) Negative/NEG – non-admin caller receives 403; (3) Boundary/STC – deleting an already-deleted patient tests the state-transition edge (double-delete); (4) Negative/DT – DELETE on a non-existent patient ID returns 404; (5) Boundary/BVA – patient ID at minimum valid length boundary is handled correctly by the delete endpoint. Each test is self-contained via fixtures that create and clean up resources.

tests.name: test_admin_soft_delete_active_patient_preserves_history
preconditions: A Patient resource exists with status active
actions: Create a Patient resource via POST /Patient with admin credentials
Record the patient ID and version
Issue GET /Patient/{id}/_history to confirm history entry exists
expected: GET /Patient/{id} returns HTTP 410 Gone or resource with meta.tag indicating deleted
GET /Patient/{id}/_history returns HTTP 200 with at least one entry
trace: R-HLT-054 – history must be retrievable after deletion
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "<base_url_fixture>"


@pytest.fixture
def admin_headers():
    return <get_auth_headers>(role="admin")


@pytest.fixture
def active_patient(admin_headers):
    payload = {"resourceType": "Patient", "active": True, "name": [{"family": "TestEP"}]}
    r = requests.post(f"{BASE_URL}/Patient", json=payload, headers=admin_headers)
    assert r.status_code in (200, 201)
    pid = r.json()["id"]
    yield pid
    # idempotent cleanup – ignore errors
    requests.delete(f"{BASE_URL}/Patient/{pid}", headers=admin_headers)


def test_admin_soft_delete_active_patient_preserves_history(admin_headers, active_patient):
    pid = active_patient
    del_r = requests.delete(f"{BASE_URL}/Patient/{pid}", headers=admin_headers)
    assert del_r.status_code in (200, 204)

    get_r = requests.get(f"{BASE_URL}/Patient/{pid}", headers=admin_headers)
    assert get_r.status_code in (410, 200)
    if get_r.status_code == 200:
        tags = [t.get("code") for t in get_r.json().get("meta", {}).get("tag", [])]
        assert "deleted" in tags

    hist_r = requests.get(f"{BASE_URL}/Patient/{pid}/_history", headers=admin_headers)
    assert hist_r.status_code == 200
    entries = hist_r.json().get("entry", [])
    assert len(entries) >= 1

tests.name: test_non_admin_delete_patient_returns_403
actions: Create a Patient resource via POST /Patient with admin credentials
Obtain auth token for a non-admin user (role:nurse)
expected: Response body contains OperationOutcome or error descriptor
Patient resource is NOT marked deleted
trace: R-HLT-054 – error pathway: 403 when not admin

tests.name: test_double_delete_already_deleted_patient
preconditions: A Patient resource exists and has already been soft-deleted
actions: Create a Patient resource via POST /Patient with admin credentials
Issue first DELETE /Patient/{id} with admin credentials (transition active→deleted)
Issue second DELETE /Patient/{id} with admin credentials (transition deleted→deleted)
expected: Second DELETE returns HTTP 200, 204, or 410 (idempotent or gone – not 500)
GET /Patient/{id}/_history still returns at least one history entry
trace: R-HLT-054 – conflicting state transition: delete of already-deleted resource
R-HLT-054 – history must survive repeated delete attempts

tests.name: test_delete_nonexistent_patient_returns_404
preconditions: The patient ID used does not exist in the system
actions: Construct a UUID-style patient ID guaranteed not to exist
expected: Response body describes the missing resource (OperationOutcome or equivalent)
trace: R-HLT-054 – missing mandatory element: resource does not exist
R-HLT-054 – negative path for invalid resource identifier

tests.name: test_delete_patient_with_minimum_length_id
preconditions: A Patient resource exists whose ID is exactly 1 character long (minimum FHIR id length)
actions: Attempt to create a Patient with id="a" (single-char minimum boundary)
If creation succeeds, issue DELETE /Patient/a with admin credentials
If creation fails (server-assigned IDs only), issue DELETE /Patient/a to confirm 404 handling
expected: If patient with id='a' exists: DELETE returns 200 or 204 and history is accessible
If patient with id='a' does not exist: DELETE returns 404
Response is never a 5xx server error
trace: R-HLT-054 – boundary value: minimum FHIR logical ID length of 1 character
R-HLT-054 – server must handle extreme ID lengths without crashing
